name: Good Neighbor Foundation
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counseling agency providing comprehensive homeownership education and mortgage delinquency prevention services at no cost.
description_long:
Good Neighbor Foundation Homeownership Center is a nonprofit organization that operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency. The organization focuses on preserving homeownership across all economic backgrounds through education and professional counseling services. Their mission centers on helping individuals achieve and maintain financial stability through housing-related guidance.

The Homeownership Center offers a comprehensive spectrum of housing counseling services tailored to different stages of homeownership. Services include pre-purchase counseling and homebuyer education workshops to prepare first-time buyers, mortgage delinquency and default resolution services for struggling homeowners, post-purchase counseling for non-delinquency situations, rental housing guidance, and disaster preparedness and recovery assistance. All services appear to be offered at no cost, consistent with their nonprofit status.

The organization distinguishes itself through its HUD approval status, which indicates regulatory oversight and adherence to federal housing counseling standards. They offer both individual counseling appointments and group education workshops, providing flexibility in how clients access services. The availability of disaster-specific counseling (both preparedness and recovery) addresses an underserved need in the housing counseling space.

A practical limitation is their limited availability—counselors are only accessible Monday through Thursday, 10am to 4pm CST via phone at 615-610-4912. The website content does not specify geographic service areas, fee structures, or detailed counselor credentials, which would be important for prospective clients evaluating fit.
pros: HUD-approved status ensures compliance with federal housing counseling standards and oversight
Comprehensive service offerings covering pre-purchase, default prevention, post-purchase, rental, and disaster-related counseling
Free counseling services available to all people regardless of financial status
Multiple delivery formats including in-person appointments, phone counseling, and group education workshops
Specialized disaster preparedness and recovery assistance programs not commonly offered
Dedicated phone line and established appointment scheduling system for client access
Nonprofit mission explicitly focused on preserving homeownership across economic backgrounds
cons: Limited availability hours (Monday-Thursday, 10am-4pm only) restricts access for working individuals
Website does not clearly specify geographic service area or whether they serve clients outside Tennessee/Nashville region
No information provided about counselor qualifications, certifications, or experience levels
No pricing transparency beyond the implication of free services, and no explanation of funding model
Limited online self-service tools or resources; appears to require phone contact for most interactions
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking comprehensive pre-purchase education and counseling before mortgage application
Homeowners facing mortgage delinquency, default, or foreclosure risk who need intervention strategies
Renters seeking housing counseling or considering homeownership transition
Communities affected by disasters needing preparedness planning or recovery assistance
diagnosis: Good Neighbor Foundation Homeownership Center is best for homebuyers, current homeowners in financial distress, or renters seeking free, HUD-approved housing guidance. Primary caveat: limited operating hours (M-Th 10am-4pm CST) and unclear geographic service boundaries may restrict accessibility for some consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 129 W Fowlkes St, Suite 129, Franklin, TN 37064
